question: Were there any board member nominees who had substantially more votes against joining than the other nominees?
gold_answer: Yes, his name is Richard A. Johnson
predicted: Yes, Richard A. Johnson had substantially more votes against joining the board than the other nominees. He received 16,105,005 votes against his election. In comparison, the other nine nominees received significantly fewer votes against, ranging from 294,935 (Virginia C. Drosos, the lowest) to 6,074,467 (Dona D. Young, the second highest). Johnson's opposition was more than 2.6 times higher than the second-highest opposition and over 54 times higher than the lowest.
